attacks.name: Cross Fusion Strike
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your Benched Fusion Strike Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

attacks.name: Max Miracle
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.effect: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 150 more damage.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Flip 2 coins. Search your deck for a number of cards up to the number of heads, put them into your hand, and shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Put up to 4 in any combination of Water Pokémon and Water Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If your Active Pokémon is your only Pokémon in play, draw 8 cards instead.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Heal 70 damage from your Active Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.
flavorText: Lysandre

rules:
Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon V, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.name: Dread End
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Darkness Pokémon in play.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Power Accelerator
attacks.effect: You may attach a Darkness Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dynamax Cannon
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, this attack does 120 more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Venomous Fang
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rising Sword
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card you have taken.

attacks.name: Buster Swing
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.effect: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.effect: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 150 more damage.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Centiferno
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you did any damage with this attack, you may attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Radiating Heat
attacks.effect: You may discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Wave
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mysterious Wave
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Weather Force
attacks.effect: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

flavorText: Its form changes depending on the weather. The rougher conditions get, the rougher Castform's disposition!

attacks.name: Thumping Snore
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Asleep. During Pokémon Checkup, flip 2 coins instead of 1. If either of them is tails, this Pokémon is still Asleep.

flavorText: This glutton appears in villages without warning and devours the entirety of their rice granaries—such occurrences have long been counted among the gravest of disasters.

attacks.name: Chain of Spirits
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 more damage for each Spiritomb in your discard pile.

flavorText: It lays curses by thinking wicked thoughts. Writings tell that this Pokémon was born out of the assembly of five score and eight malevolent spirits.

attacks.name: Very Vulnerable
attacks.effect: If you have no cards in your hand, this attack does 150 more damage.

flavorText: Snaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase as it all but dances around them.

attacks.name: Resolute Spite
attacks.effect: Put up to 7 damage counters on this Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter you placed in this way.

attacks.name: Eerie Light
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Resentment at being cast off made it spring into being. Some say that treating it well will satisfy it, and it will once more become a stuffed toy.

attacks.name: Screaming Circle
attacks.effect: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Possesses potential victims' shadows in an effort to steal away the victims' lives. If your shadow begins to laugh, you must take hold of a protective charm posthaste!

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Possesses cheek sacs in which it stores electricity. This clever forest-dweller roasts tough berries with an electric shock before consuming them.
This Pokémon haunts dilapidated mansions. It sways its arms to hypnotize opponents with the ominous dancing of its flames.

attacks.name: Royal Blaze
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Leon card in your discard pile.

flavorText: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

attacks.name: Poisonous Whip
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Assassin's Rose
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: After captivating opponents with its sweet scent, it lashes them with its thorny whips.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: Mushroom-lacking specimens of this Pokémon lie unmoving in the forest, lending credence to the hypothesis that the large mushroom is in control of Parasect's actions.

rules:
You can use this card only if you put another card from your hand in the Lost Zone. Choose a Pokémon Tool attached to any Pokémon, or any Stadium in play, and put it in the Lost Zone.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a basic Darkness Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Darkness Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Each player can't have more than 4 Benched Pokémon. If a player has 5 or more Benched Pokémon, they discard Benched Pokémon until they have 4 Pokémon on the Bench. The player who played this card discards first. If more than one effect changes the number of Benched Pokémon allowed, use the smaller number.

If the Pokémon V this card is attached to has full HP and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

attacks.name: Ticking Curse
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Lost Impact
attacks.effect: Put 2 Energy attached to your Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

attacks.name: Star Requiem
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if you have 10 or more cards in the Lost Zone. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Discard 1 of your Benched Pokémon V and all attached cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Choose a Basic Pokémon in your discard pile and switch it with 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck and discard any number of Item cards you find there. Your opponent shuffles the other cards back into their deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for up to 4 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 2 cards. If your Active Pokémon has "Hisuian" in its name, draw 2 more cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You can use this card only if you have 10 or more cards in the Lost Zone. During your opponent's next turn, all of your Pokémon take 120 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon V (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (This includes Pokémon that come into play during that turn.)

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put 3 of them into your hand. Put the other cards in the Lost Zone.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for up to 3 Evolution Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy. If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.

The Pokémon this card is attached to can attack even if it's Asleep or Paralyzed.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Discard 1 of your Benched Pokémon V and all attached cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal any number of Pokémon Tool cards you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Choose a Basic Pokémon in your discard pile and switch it with 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Reveal the top 5 cards of your deck and have your opponent choose 2 of them. Discard the chosen cards and put the remaining cards into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Prevent all damage done to the Pokémon this card is attached to by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that have 40 HP or less remaining.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck and discard any number of Item cards you find there. Your opponent shuffles the other cards back into their deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You can use this card only if you have 7 or more cards in the Lost Zone. Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards of different types and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

You can use this card only if you put another card from your hand in the Lost Zone. Choose a Pokémon Tool attached to any Pokémon, or any Stadium in play, and put it in the Lost Zone.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Whenever a Pokémon (either yours or your opponent's) is Knocked Out, put that Pokémon in the Lost Zone instead of the discard pile. (Discard all attached cards.)
All Pokémon that have any Water or Fighting Energy attached (both yours and your opponent's) take 20 less damage from attacks from the opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

Search your deck for up to 4 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 2 cards. If your Active Pokémon has "Hisuian" in its name, draw 2 more cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You can use this card only if you have 10 or more cards in the Lost Zone. During your opponent's next turn, all of your Pokémon take 120 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon V (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (This includes Pokémon that come into play during that turn.)

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Move up to 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put 3 of them into your hand. Put the other cards in the Lost Zone.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

If the Pokémon V this card is attached to has full HP and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Search your deck for up to 3 Evolution Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Look at the top card of your deck. You may switch that card with 1 of your face-down Prize cards. (The cards stay face down.)

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Spill Out
attacks.effect: Discard your hand. If you discarded 5 or more cards in this way, this attack does 150 more damage.

flavorText: It stashes berries in its tail—so many berries that they fall out constantly. But this Pokémon is a bit slow-witted, so it doesn't notice the loss.
Found throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon becomes uneasy if its cheeks are ever completely empty of berries.

attacks.name: Collapse
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It remains asleep from birth to death as a result of the sedative properties of the leaves that form its diet.

attacks.name: Lost Headbutt
attacks.effect: Put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: These Pokémon can crush a car with no more than a headbutt. Bouffalant with more hair on their heads hold higher positions within the herd.

attacks.name: Ticking Curse
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Void Return
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Cyclone
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: All-You-Can-Grab
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. Search your deck for a number of cards up to the number of heads and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It uses its tails for everything. If it wraps both of its tails around you and gives you a squeeze, that's proof it really likes you.

attacks.name: Mischievous Tail
attacks.effect: Look at the top card of your opponent's deck. You may have your opponent shuffle their deck.

flavorText: This treetop dweller possesses a tail as dexterous as a hand. Ancient writings describe this Pokémon as a one-armed oddity.

attacks.name: Thumping Snore
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Asleep. During Pokémon Checkup, flip 2 coins instead of 1. If either of them is tails, this Pokémon is still Asleep.

flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

attacks.name: Downgrading Beam
attacks.effect: Devolve 1 of your opponent's evolved Pokémon by removing any number of Evolution cards from it. Your opponent shuffles those cards into their deck.

flavorText: Porygon-Z had a program installed to allow it to move between dimensions, but the program also caused instability in Porygon-Z's behavior.

attacks.name: Garbage Attack
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon Tool card in the Lost Zone (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: This is a Porygon that was updated with special data. Porygon2 develops itself by learning about many different subjects all on its own.

attacks.name: Branch Calculation
attacks.effect: Look at the top 4 cards of either player's deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: State-of-the-art technology was used to create Porygon. It was the first artificial Pokémon to be created via computer programming.
Lickilicky's strange tongue can stretch to many times the length of its body. No one has figured out how Lickilicky's tongue can stretch so far.
If this Pokémon's sticky saliva gets on you and you don't clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won't go away, either.

attacks.name: Flight Surf
attacks.effect: If you have a Stadium in play, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 80 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Rolling Shell
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy—it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.

attacks.name: Rigidify
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: A creature given to melancholy. I suspect its metallic shell developed as a result of the mucus on its skin reacting with the iron in Hisui's water.
Goomy hides away in the shade of trees, where it's nice and humid. If the slime coating its body dries out, the Pokémon instantly becomes lethargic.

attacks.effect: Put 2 Energy attached to your Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if you have 10 or more cards in the Lost Zone. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

attacks.name: Windup Beam
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 60 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Built roughly 500 years ago by a scientist, the part called the Soul-Heart is the actual life-form.

attacks.name: Field Trap
attacks.effect: If your opponent has a Stadium in play, discard it. If you discarded a Stadium in this way, discard 2 Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Living in mud with a high iron content has given it a strong steel body.

attacks.name: Gravitational Drop
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: Some believe that its bell-like cry opens holes to another world. It has been revered as a deity since ancient times.
It appears in ancient ruins. The pattern on its body doesn't come from any culture in the Galar region, so it remains shrouded in mystery.

attacks.name: Energy Stream
attacks.effect: Attach up to 2 Metal Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Destructive Finish
attacks.effect: Discard cards from the top of your deck until only 1 card remains. This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy card you discarded in this way.

rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.
flavorText: It is said that if an Onix lives for over 100 years, its composition changes to become diamond-like.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.
flavorText: Because of Sneasler's virulent poison and daunting physical prowess, no other species could hope to best it on the frozen highlands. Preferring solitude, this species does not form packs.

attacks.name: Hand of Djinn
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Darkness Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It gathers things it likes and passes them through its loop to teleport them to a secret place.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It spins while making its luminescent spots flash. These spots allow it to communicate with others by using different patterns of light.

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Pitch-Black Blade
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: On a moonless night, a strange incident occurred in which every one of a village's inhabitants suffered nightmares. The villagers attested that Darkrai appeared before them in these nightmares.

attacks.name: Big Bang Arm
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dynamic Tail
attacks.effect: This attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Chain of Spirits
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 more damage for each Spiritomb in your discard pile.

flavorText: Exactly 108 spirits gathered to become this Pokémon. Apparently there are some ill-natured spirits in the mix.

attacks.name: Sucker Punch and Turn
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Darkness Pokémon.

flavorText: Constant polishing makes the edge of the blade on its tail extremely sharp. It's Zangoose's archrival.

attacks.name: Night Cyclone
attacks.effect: Move all Energy from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Its goons take care of most of the fighting for it. The only time it dirties its own hands is in delivering a final blow to finish off an opponent.
It searches for shiny things for its boss. Murkrow's presence is said to be unlucky, so many people detest it.

attacks.name: String Bind
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Venomous Fang
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It spews threads from its mouth to catch its prey. When night falls, it leaves its web to go hunt aggressively.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Although the poison from its fangs isn't that strong, it's potent enough to weaken prey that gets caught in its web.

attacks.name: Power Stone
attacks.effect: Attach up to 2 Fighting Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Lost Shot
attacks.effect: Put the top card of your opponent's deck in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: It stands in grasslands, watching the sun's descent from zenith to horizon. This Pokémon has a talent for delivering dynamic kicks.

attacks.name: Fighting Headbutt
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness. If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: Five of them are troopers, and one is the brass. The brass's orders are absolute.

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: This Pokémon can bond very strongly with its Trainer, but it also has a habit of biting. Raising a Rockruff for a long time can be challenging.

attacks.name: Lucky Find
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When beset by attackers, Carbink wipes them all out by firing high-energy beams from the gems embedded in its body.
Seven Binacle come together to form one Barbaracle. The Binacle that serves as the head gives orders to those serving as the limbs.
After two Binacle find a suitably sized rock, they adhere themselves to it and live together. They cooperate to gather food during high tide.

attacks.name: Smashing Edge
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: From the forces of lightning and wind, it creates energy to give nutrients to the soil and make the land abundant.

attacks.name: Double Smash
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

flavorText: When Mienshao comes across a truly challenging opponent, it will lighten itself by biting off the fur on its arms.
In one minute, a well-trained Mienfoo can chop with its arms more than 100 times.

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon and heal all damage from it.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its search for food sometimes leads it onto land, where it leaves behind a sticky trail of slime as it passes through.

attacks.name: Into the Deep
attacks.effect: Put up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Rock-hard scales and oil-filled swim bladders allow this Pokémon to survive the intense water pressure of the deep sea.

attacks.name: Battle Step
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Fighting Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe's next move.

attacks.name: Focus Fist
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It eats just one berry a day. By enduring hunger, its spirit is tempered and made sharper.

attacks.name: Shove
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Muscular Slap
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Hariyama that are big and fat aren't necessarily strong. There are some small ones that move nimbly and use moves skillfully.

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: There's a rumor of a traditional recipe for stew that Trainers can use to raise strong Makuhita.

attacks.name: Hurricane Shock
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads. If at least 2 of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey... Then its elongated fangs do the rest.

attacks.name: Cyclone Pincers
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It flies straight at its target's face, then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

attacks.name: Joust
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Impound
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Though it pretends to be a tree, it fails to fool even children. To the touch, its body feels more like rock than tree bark. Sudowoodo's extreme aversion to water merits special note.

attacks.name: Lost Dive
attacks.effect: Put the top 3 cards of your deck in the Lost Zone.

attacks.name: Ancient Star
attacks.effect: Until this Pokémon leaves play, it gains an Ability that has the effect "Your opponent's Pokémon V in play, except any Aerodactyl VSTAR, have no Abilities." (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rock Crush
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Geo Cannon
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is an Energy card, this attack does 100 more damage, and attach that card to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rocky Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: It can load up to three projectiles per arm into the holes in its hands. What launches out of those holes could be either rocks or Roggenrola.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: It begins walking on its hind legs after evolution. It can punch holes through boulders with its horn.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging tackles.

attacks.name: Strong-Arm Lariat
attacks.effect: You may do 100 more damage. If you do, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.
A sturdy creature boasting a robust physique and boundless stamina. Loves training above all else and voluntarily assists with tasks such as construction and clearing land.
Its whole body is composed of muscles. Even though it's the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.

attacks.name: Split Spiral Punch
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Splash Loop
attacks.effect: Put 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: Its body is solid muscle. When swimming through cold seas, Poliwrath uses its impressive arms to smash through drift ice and plow forward.

attacks.name: Very Vulnerable
attacks.effect: If you have no cards in your hand, this attack does 150 more damage.

flavorText: Snaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase as it all but dances around them.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: They patrol their territory in pairs. I believe the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.

attacks.name: Blossom Tail
attacks.effect: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Night Footsteps
attacks.effect: Choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon and put 2 damage counters on each of them.

attacks.name: Phantom Strike
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Phantom Strike.

flavorText: It probes its surroundings with all its senses save one—it doesn't use its sense of sight. Spectrier's kicks are said to separate soul from body.

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Worst Gift
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on all of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It wears a rag fashioned into a Pikachu costume in an effort to look less scary. Unfortunately, the costume only makes it creepier.
Comfey picks flowers with its vine and decorates itself with them. For some reason, flowers won't wither once they're attached to a Comfey.

attacks.name: Synchro Kinesis
attacks.effect: Each player reveals their hand. If a card in your opponent's hand has the same name as a card in your hand, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Gazing at its luminescent spots will quickly induce a hypnotic state, putting the observer under Malamar's control.

attacks.name: Fickle Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It spins while making its luminescent spots flash. These spots allow it to communicate with others by using different patterns of light.

attacks.name: Doom Curse
attacks.effect: At the end of your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon will be Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Call Back
attacks.effect: Put a card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: With its disheveled white fur, it looks like an embodiment of death. Heedless of its own safety, Zoroark attacks its nemeses with a bitter energy so intense, it lacerates Zoroark's own body.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: A once-departed soul, returned to life in Hisui. Derives power from resentment, which rises as energy atop its head and takes on the forms of foes. In this way, Zorua vents lingering malice.

attacks.name: Moonglow Reverse
attacks.effect: Move 2 damage counters from each of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Those who sleep holding Cresselia's feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.
Resentment at being cast off made it spring into being. Some say that treating it well will satisfy it, and it will once more become a stuffed toy.
There's a proverb that says, "Shun the house where Shuppet gather in the growing dusk."

attacks.name: Tempting Trap
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat. During your next turn, the Defending Pokémon takes 90 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.

attacks.name: Lost Mine
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if you have 10 or more cards in the Lost Zone. Put 12 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: This Pokémon is feared. When its gemstone eyes begin to glow with a sinister shine, it's believed that Sableye will steal people's spirits away.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Radiant Pokémon Rule: You can't have more than 1 Radiant Pokémon in your deck.
flavorText: It has the power to predict the future. Its power peaks when it is protecting its Trainer.

attacks.name: Alluring Dance
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

attacks.name: Tricky Slap
attacks.effect: You and your opponent play Rock-Paper-Scissors until someone wins. If you win, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The behavior of this clown-like Pokémon reminds one of pantomime. It creates invisible walls using a force emitted from its fingertips.

attacks.name: Screaming Circle
attacks.effect: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: On the night of a full moon, if shadows move on their own and laugh, it must be Gengar's doing.

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.effect: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: This frightful, malevolent spirit can glide through walls, appearing wherever it likes. According to rumor, victims of a Haunter's lick will wither to death day by day.

attacks.name: Furtive Drop
attacks.effect: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Born from gases, anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.
A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

attacks.name: Wonder Storm
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each Psychic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It can be found in quiet mountain areas on a full moon's night. Its dancing and its tiny, faintly glowing wings confer upon it a lovely fairylike quality.

attacks.name: Coil
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's attacks do 120 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Extreme Current
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.
These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

attacks.name: Call Sign
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Lightning Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: One alone can emit only a trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.

attacks.name: Scrap Short
attacks.effect: Put any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile in the Lost Zone. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you put in the Lost Zone in this way.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Magnetic Grip
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Electro Star
attacks.effect: This attack does 90 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Magnetic Tension
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Splitting Beam
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Assault Laser
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It stimulates its own muscles with electricity, so it can move quickly. It eases its soreness with electricity, too, so it can recover quickly as well.

attacks.name: Thunder Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Ace Spark
attacks.effect: If you have used your VSTAR Power, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

attacks.name: Freeze Down
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Glastrier emits intense cold from its hooves. It's also a belligerent Pokémon—anything it wants, it takes by force.

attacks.name: Spit Innocently
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

flavorText: It's so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it's battling midfight.

attacks.name: Max Frost
attacks.effect: You may discard any amount of Water Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rapid Freeze
attacks.effect: Attach any number of Water Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.
They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.

attacks.name: Upstream Spirits
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each basic Energy card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those cards into your deck.

attacks.name: Water Shot
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Clads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species throughout all Hisui's rivers is Basculegion's equal.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Though it differs from other Basculin in several respects, including demeanor—this one is gentle—I have categorized it as a regional form given the vast array of shared qualities.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: If it sees any packs of Darumaka going after Snover, it chases them off, swinging its sizable arms like hammers.
One is likely to encounter this Pokémon while out in the snow. There are stories of Snover appearing in human settlements but doing no harm—rather, they bond with the children.

attacks.name: Swim Freely
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: They traverse the deep waters as if crawling over the seafloor. The fantastic lights of its fins shine like stars in the night sky.
It lures in prey with its shining tail fins. It stays near the surface during the day and moves to the depths when night falls.
There's speculation that its appearance is determined by what it eats, but the truth remains elusive.

attacks.name: Matching Look
attacks.effect: Each player reveals the top 2 cards of their deck, then draws those cards.

flavorText: Luvdisc makes its home in coral reefs in warm seas. It especially likes sleeping in the space between Corsola's branches.
With the arrival of a storm at sea, this Pokémon will show itself on the surface. When a Kingdra and a Dragonite meet, a fierce battle ensues.

attacks.name: Swim Freely
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Jet
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It's the males that raise the offspring. While Seadra are raising young, the spines on their backs secrete thicker and stronger poison.

attacks.name: Reverse Thrust
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Horsea makes its home in oceans with gentle currents. If this Pokémon is under attack, it spits out pitch-black ink and escapes.

attacks.name: Swim Freely
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Floe Return
attacks.effect: Shuffle any amount of Water Energy from your Pokémon into your deck. This attack does 40 damage for each card you shuffled into your deck in this way.

flavorText: It sunbathes on the beach after meals. The rise in its body temperature helps its digestion.
Thanks to its thick fat, cold seas don't bother it at all, but it gets tired pretty easily in warm waters.

attacks.name: Lordly Songleader
attacks.effect: If Poliwag is on your Bench, this attack does 60 more damage. If Poliwhirl is on your Bench, this attack does 90 more damage. If Poliwrath is on your Bench, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: At nightfall, these Pokémon appear on the shores of lakes. They announce their territorial claims by letting out cries that sound like shouting.

attacks.name: Double Smash
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Staring at the swirl on its belly causes drowsiness. This trait of Poliwhirl's has been used in place of lullabies to get children to go to sleep.

attacks.name: Splashing Dodge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: For Poliwag, swimming is easier than walking. The swirl pattern on its belly is actually part of the Pokémon's innards showing through the skin.

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: The temperature of its breath is over 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, but Pyroar doesn't use it on its prey. This Pokémon prefers to eat raw meat.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: This hot-blooded Pokémon is filled with curiosity. When it gets angry or starts fighting, its short mane gets hot.

attacks.name: Eerie Glow
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

attacks.name: Magical Fire
attacks.effect: Put 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon in the Lost Zone. This attack also does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: This Pokémon haunts dilapidated mansions. It sways its arms to hypnotize opponents with the ominous dancing of its flames.

attacks.name: Flickering Glow
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears just before someone passes away, so it's feared as an emissary of death.

attacks.name: Kindling Panic
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: The flame on its head keeps its body slightly warm. This Pokémon takes lost children by the hand to guide them to the spirit world.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows out black soot if it is endangered.

attacks.name: Lost Volcano
attacks.effect: Put all Energy attached to this Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: Its body is as hot as lava and is always billowing. Flames will occasionally burst from its shell.

attacks.name: Draw In
attacks.effect: Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is made of magma. If it doesn't keep moving, its body will cool and harden.

attacks.name: Mysterious Wave
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It's famous for its high level of intelligence, and the large size of its brain is proof that it also possesses immense psychic power.

attacks.name: Barrier Attack
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It barely moves, but it's still alive. Hiding in its shell without food or water seems to have awakened its psychic powers.
A constant collector of information, this Pokémon is very smart. Very strong is what it isn't.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: People fear it due to a belief that it devours any who try to cut down trees in its forest, but to the Pokémon it shares its woods with, it's kind.
After a lost child perished in the forest, their spirit possessed a tree stump, causing the spirit's rebirth as this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poisonous Whip
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Assassin's Rose
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: After captivating opponents with its sweet scent, it lashes them with its thorny whips.

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its flowers give off a relaxing fragrance. The stronger its aroma, the healthier the Roselia is.

attacks.name: Fan Tornado
attacks.effect: You may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tearing Gust
attacks.effect: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: A Pokémon that was feared as a forest guardian. It can read the foe's mind and take preemptive action.

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.effect: You may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If it remains still, it looks just like a real nut. It delights in surprising foraging Pokémon.

attacks.name: Nadir Powder
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 8 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

flavorText: Tends to be drawn to bonfires on dark nights. Difficult to chase away from settlements because of the way it scatters highly toxic scales.

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks if that damage is 60 or less.

flavorText: It never forgets any attack it endured while in the cocoon. After evolution, it seeks payback.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has an aggressive nature. It stabs prey with its long, narrow mouth to drain the prey's fluids.

attacks.name: Entangling String
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It drinks dew that collects on its silk and waits for evolution. Its hard cocoon repels attacks.

attacks.name: Creepy-Crawly Congregation
attacks.effect: Search your deck for any number of Wurmple, Silcoon, Beautifly, Cascoon, and Dustox, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It lives among the tall grass and in forests. It repels attacks by raising up the spikes on its rear.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: The bug is mostly dead, with the mushroom on its back having become the main body. If the mushroom comes off, the bug stops moving.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The mushrooms, known as tochukaso, are controlling the bug. Even if the bug bugs the mushrooms, they tell it to bug off.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Allergy Storm
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Supporter cards from their hand. If tails, during your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

flavorText: It has the world's largest petals. With every step, the petals shake out heavy clouds of toxic pollen.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.
If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move. It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.
